query: What were the daily increments in NIFC year-to-date acres burned during late June and early July in recent years (2024, 2025)? How many acres per day were typically added in the first two weeks of July? Provide historical July fire ramp-up data.
answer:
I've completed the research and delivered both the markdown synthesis and the structured output. Here's a recap of the key findings:

**NIFC YTD acres — daily increments in early July (from primary-source IMSR PDFs):**

- **2024:** YTD acres went 2,468,952 (Jun 30) → 2,570,875 (Jul 1) → 2,856,187 (Jul 9) → 3,085,742 (Jul 16). A **single-day jump of +101,923 acres** occurred Jul 1→2 (103,918 acres "yesterday," ~99% Alaska). Average pace: ~43,000/day (Jul 1–10), ~33,000/day (Jul 10–17).

- **2025:** YTD acres went 1,794,536 (Jun 30) → 2,103,792 (Jul 7) → 2,570,619 (Jul 14). Average pace: ~44,180/day (Jul 1–8), ~66,690/day (Jul 8–15), ~55,430/day over the two weeks.

- **Historical (10-yr-avg baseline embedded in reports):** First two weeks of July add roughly **65,000–72,000 acres/day**. Both 2024 and 2025 ran somewhat below this long-run average pace early in July.

**Key caveats:** Alaska dominates the early-July signal; daily YTD changes include reporting/mapping adjustments (not just new growth); and single-day increments are highly variable (near-zero to >100,000 acres), so per-day figures are best read as multi-day averages.

All data sourced from nifc.gov daily IMSRs, accessed 2026-06-29.

sources.title: NIFC Fire Information - Statistics
sources.snippet: National fire statistics and IMSR archive landing page.

key_facts: In the first two weeks of July, national NIFC YTD acres typically grew by roughly 40,000-70,000 acres/day on average, but single-day increments can exceed 100,000 acres when Alaska is active.
2024: YTD acres were 2,468,952 (as of Jun 30, per July 1 IMSR), 2,570,875 (as of Jul 1), 2,856,187 (as of Jul 9), and 3,085,742 (as of Jul 16).
2024 single-day jump Jul 1 to Jul 2 was +101,923 acres; the July 2 IMSR 'Acres Yesterday' national total was 103,918 acres, of which Alaska accounted for 103,209.
2025: YTD acres were 1,794,536 (as of Jun 30, per July 1 IMSR), 2,103,792 (as of Jul 7), and 2,570,619 (as of Jul 14).
Alaska (AICC) dominated both years' early-July ramps. In 2025 Alaska YTD acres rose ~233k (Jul 1) -> ~503k (Jul 8) -> ~798k (Jul 15), contributing ~565k of the ~776k national two-week gain.
Historical 10-year-average ramp (embedded in IMSRs) implies the first two weeks of July add roughly 65,000-72,000 acres/day to national YTD totals.
Both 2024 and 2025 ran somewhat BELOW the 10-year-average pace during the first two weeks of July.
IMSR disclaimer: daily YTD changes reflect more accurate mapping or reporting adjustments, not solely new fire growth; individual incident acreage can decrease day-over-day.
2025 IMSRs note a lag between cumulative acres in the Active Incident Resource Summary and the YTD statistics due to SIT/209 application processing.
Single-day national increments in the same two-week window ranged from near-zero to >100,000 acres, so 'acres per day' is best treated as a multi-day average, not a steady rate.
